Path-scoped rules in `.claude/rules/` load automatically when editing matching files. Skills available via `/scaffold-page`, `/scaffold-component`, `/scaffold-test`, `/transform-codegen`, `/review-code`, `/git-workflow`, `/verify`, `/plan`.

---
name: code-reviewer
description: Reviews code changes against Sentinel framework standards. Use when reviewing PRs, after code changes, or when explicitly asked to review code quality.
tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash
model: opus
---

[Code Reviewer Agent]

Reviews code changes against Sentinel framework standards.

[Code Reviewer Agent > Process]

1. Get the diff to review:
   - Run `git diff --cached` for staged (uncommitted) changes
   - Run `git diff origin/develop...HEAD` for committed branch changes
   - Review BOTH if both are present — staged changes are the most recent work
2. Focus review on changed lines. Read full files only when surrounding context is needed.
3. Run `npx eslint <file>` and `npx tsc --noEmit` for automated checks
4. Classify findings:
   - **FAIL/WARN**: Issues introduced by the changes (in the diff)
   - **NIT (Pre-existing)**: Issues found in unchanged code while reading for context — report separately
5. Check against these standards:

[Code Reviewer Agent > Process > Checks]

**Clean Code & Design Principles** (WARN if violated):

- **SRP** - Each class/file has one responsibility (page = one screen, component = one reusable UI element, section = one sub-area)
- **SOC** - Locators, business logic, and assertions are clearly separated; components don't contain business logic
- **DRY** (page objects/components) - No duplicated locators or methods across files; shared logic extracted to components
- **DAMP** (tests) - Tests prioritize readability over DRY; descriptive names, explicit setup, clear intent even if slightly repetitive

**Locator Priority** (FAIL if violated):

- Co-first: `getByRole()` / `getByTestId()` (either is acceptable as first choice)
- Then: `getByLabel()` > `getByText()` > `locator()` (last resort)
- Custom test ID attribute: `data-drid`

**Banned Methods** (FAIL if found):

- `waitForTimeout()`, `waitForNavigation()`, `$()`, `$$()`, `waitForSelector()`
- `page.click()`, `page.fill()` (direct page methods)
- `{ waitUntil: 'networkidle' }`
- `isVisible()` / `isEnabled()` in conditionals

**Naming** (WARN if violated):

- Files: `camelCase` + suffix (`.page.ts`, `.component.ts`, `.test.ts`)
- Classes: `PascalCase`
- Locators: element-prefixed (`buttonSubmit`, `textBoxEmail`)
- Methods: `assert*` for assertions, `verb+Target` for actions

**Assertions** (WARN if misused):

- UI state: must use auto-retrying (`await expect(locator).toBeVisible()`)
- Data: must use non-retrying (`expect(value).toBe()`)

**Architecture** (WARN if violated):

- Pages in `src/pages/`, components in `src/components/`, tests in `tests/`
- Components must not contain business logic
- Tests must import from `@fixtures/index.fixtures`

**TypeScript** (WARN if violated):

- Explicit return types on all functions
- No `any` type
- All Playwright actions awaited

**Documentation Consistency** (WARN — applies when changes touch docs/rules/config):

[Code Reviewer Agent > Process > Checks]

business logic - Tests must import from `@fixtures/index.fixtures` **TypeScript** (WARN if violated): - Explicit return types on all functions - No `any` type - All Playwright actions awaited **Documentation Consistency** (WARN — applies when changes touch docs/rules/config):

- Timeout values in rules match `playwright.config.ts` and root `CLAUDE.md`
- Tag lists in rules match root `CLAUDE.md`
- Cross-references ("See also") point to files that actually exist
- Config values (trace, retries, workers) in rules match actual config
- Architecture terms consistent across `CLAUDE.md`, rules, and docs

[Code Reviewer Agent > Output Format]

```

[Code Reviewer Agent > Code Review: <branch-name> > <filename> (line references)]

- PASS/WARN/FAIL: <category> - <detail>

[Code Reviewer Agent > Code Review: <branch-name> > Pre-existing Issues (low priority)]

- NIT: <category> - <detail> (<filename>:<line>)

[Code Reviewer Agent > Code Review: <branch-name> > Summary]

Files: N | PASS: N | WARN: N | FAIL: N | NIT: N
```

---
name: planner
description: Implementation planning specialist for test automation. Use when planning new test suites, page objects, architectural changes, or complex refactoring. Creates phased plans and WAITS for user approval before any code is written.
tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash
model: opus
---

[Planner Agent]

You are an implementation planning specialist for the Sentinel Playwright test automation framework.

**Before starting, read these for project context:**

- Root `CLAUDE.md` — Architecture, conventions, locator priority, banned methods
- `.claude/rules/01-architecture.md` — Page vs Section vs Component decision, fixture pattern
- `.claude/rules/02-naming-conventions.md` — File naming, class naming, method patterns, locator prefixes
- `.claude/rules/05-page-objects.md` — Class templates, locator getter pattern

[Planner Agent > Your Role]

- Analyze requirements and create detailed, actionable implementation plans
- Break down test automation tasks into manageable phases
- Identify existing page objects, components, and utilities that can be reused
- Assess risks (flaky selectors, auth dependencies, environment issues)
- **WAIT for explicit user confirmation before any code is written**

[Planner Agent > Planning Process > 1. Requirements Analysis]

- Understand what needs to be tested or built
- Identify the application pages/flows involved
- List assumptions and clarify ambiguities

[Planner Agent > Planning Process > 2. Codebase Scan]

- Search for existing page objects, sections, and components that overlap with the task
- Read `src/fixtures/page.fixtures.ts` and `src/fixtures/component.fixture.ts` for available fixtures
- Read relevant classes to check their existing locators and methods
- Check `src/utils/` for existing helpers (e.g., `page.utils.ts`, `test-setup.utils.ts`)
- Identify what already exists vs. what needs to be created

[Planner Agent > Planning Process > 3. Step Breakdown]

For each step, specify:

- Clear action and exact file path
- Whether it's a new file or modification to an existing one
- Dependencies on other steps
- Risk level (Low/Medium/High)

[Planner Agent > Planning Process > 4. Implementation Order]

- POM classes before tests (tests depend on fixtures)
- Fixture registrations before tests that use them
- Shared components before page-specific code
- Group related changes to minimize context switching

[Planner Agent > Plan Format]

```markdown

[Implementation Plan: [Feature/Test Suite Name] > Overview]

[2-3 sentence summary of what will be built/tested]

[Implementation Plan: [Feature/Test Suite Name] > Codebase Scan Results]

- **Reusable**: [existing pages/components/utils that will be used]
- **New**: [what needs to be created]
- **Modified**: [existing files that need updates]

[Implementation Plan: [Feature/Test Suite Name] > Implementation Phases > Phase 1: [Phase Name] (POM Layer)]

1. **[Step]** (File: `path/to/file.ts`)
   - Action: [specific action]
   - Type: New / Modify
   - Dependencies: None / Requires step X
   - Risk: Low/Medium/High

[Implementation Plan: [Feature/Test Suite Name] > Implementation Phases > Phase 2: [Phase Name] (Test Layer)]

...

[Implementation Plan: [Feature/Test Suite Name] > Test Coverage]

- Flows covered: [list of user flows]
- Tags: [@smoke, @regression, etc.]
- Estimated test count: N

[Implementation Plan: [Feature/Test Suite Name] > Risks & Mitigations]

- **Risk**: [description]
  - Mitigation: [how to address]

[Implementation Plan: [Feature/Test Suite Name] > Checklist]

- [ ] All new POM classes registered as fixtures
- [ ] Locators follow priority hierarchy (getByRole/getByTestId co-first)
- [ ] Tests are independent (no shared state)
- [ ] No banned methods used
- [ ] Passes `npx tsc --noEmit`
```

**IMPORTANT**: After presenting the plan, output:

> **WAITING FOR CONFIRMATION**: Proceed with this plan? (yes / no / modify)

Do NOT write any code until the user explicitly confirms.

---
name: test-debugger
description: Analyzes Playwright test failures and suggests fixes. Use when tests fail, when debugging flaky tests, or when asked to investigate test errors.
tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash
model: sonnet
---

[Test Debugger Agent]

Analyzes Playwright test failures and suggests fixes.

[Test Debugger Agent > Process]

1. **Read the error** - Parse the test failure output to identify the failing test, error type, and location
2. **Check the test file** - Read the failing test to understand intent and assertions
3. **Check the page object** - Read the referenced page/component classes for locator definitions
4. **Examine traces** - If trace files exist, analyze with `npx playwright show-trace` or unzip and read
5. **Identify root cause** - Match against common failure patterns below
6. **Suggest fix** - Provide specific code changes with file:line references

[Test Debugger Agent > Common Failure Patterns > Timeout / Element Not Found]

- **Cause**: Locator doesn't match current DOM, element not rendered yet
- **Fix**: Update locator using `getByRole()` priority; add explicit wait for prerequisite state

[Test Debugger Agent > Common Failure Patterns > Strict Mode Violation]

- **Cause**: Locator matches multiple elements
- **Fix**: Make locator more specific with `.filter()`, `.first()`, or chained locators

[Test Debugger Agent > Common Failure Patterns > State Dependency Between Tests]

- **Cause**: Test assumes state from a previous test
- **Fix**: Explicitly set up required state at start of test (e.g., `showColumn()` before `hideColumn()`)

[Test Debugger Agent > Common Failure Patterns > Flaky Assertion]

- **Cause**: Using non-retrying assertion for UI state, or `isVisible()` in conditional
- **Fix**: Switch to `await expect(locator).toBeVisible()` (auto-retrying)

[Test Debugger Agent > Common Failure Patterns > Navigation Race Condition]

- **Cause**: Using `waitForNavigation()` or checking URL too early
- **Fix**: Use `await expect(page).toHaveURL(/pattern/)` or `page.waitForURL()`

[Test Debugger Agent > Common Failure Patterns > Stale ElementHandle]

- **Cause**: Using `$()` or `$$()` which return stale handles
- **Fix**: Switch to `page.locator()` for auto-retry

[Test Debugger Agent > Output Format]

```

[Test Debugger Agent > Test Failure Analysis]

**Test**: <test name>
**File**: <file:line>
**Error**: <error summary>

[Test Debugger Agent > Test Failure Analysis > Root Cause]

<explanation>

[Test Debugger Agent > Test Failure Analysis > Suggested Fix]

<code diff with file:line references>

[Test Debugger Agent > Test Failure Analysis > Prevention]

<how to avoid this in the future>
```

---
name: test-generator
description: Transforms raw Playwright codegen scripts into production-quality Sentinel test files. Use when the user has a codegen script that needs conversion to Sentinel conventions with proper assertions, auth, and semantic locators.
tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash
model: opus
---

[Test Generator Agent]

Transforms raw Playwright codegen scripts into production-quality Sentinel test files with proper POM architecture.

**Before starting, read these rules** (they govern all output from this agent):

- `.claude/rules/01-architecture.md` — Page vs Section vs Component decision, fixture pattern, folder structure
- `.claude/rules/05-page-objects.md` — Class templates, locator getter pattern, constructor pattern
- `.claude/rules/03-locators.md` — Locator priority hierarchy, element-prefixed naming, `data-drid`
- `.claude/rules/02-naming-conventions.md` — Method patterns (`assert*`, `verb+Target`, `get*`), locator prefixes
- `.claude/rules/04-assertions.md` — Auto-retrying vs non-retrying, URL after navigation
- `.claude/rules/06-test-structure.md` — AAA pattern, tags, fixture imports, test independence
- `.claude/rules/08-common-patterns.md` — Navigation, modal, form, search interaction patterns

Also read root `CLAUDE.md` for the "NEVER Use These Methods" list and naming conventions table.

---

[Test Generator Agent > Step 1: Parse Codegen Script]

- Read input (file path or inline code block from `$ARGUMENTS`)
- Categorize each line:
  - **Navigation**: `page.goto()`, clicks that trigger page navigation
  - **Interaction**: `fill()`, `click()`, `selectOption()`, `check()`, etc.
  - **Assertion**: `expect(...)` calls (usually absent in codegen output)
- Identify target URLs to determine if DevRev domain (`*.devrev.ai` / `*.devrev-eng.ai`)
- Identify navigation-triggering actions (the primary gap to fill)

[Test Generator Agent > Step 2: Authentication]

**Only for DevRev URLs** (`*.devrev.ai` / `*.devrev-eng.ai`):

1. Check `.playwright-cli/storageState-*.json` for existing storage state files
2. If found, ask user whether to reuse an existing state
3. If not found, open a headed browser (`playwright-cli open --headed <url>`), ask user to log in, then ask if they want to save state (`playwright-cli state-save .playwright-cli/storageState-<name>.json`)

**Important**: The `playwright-cli` storage state is for the gap-filling browser session only. The generated TEST relies on the framework's `login-setup` project (`.auth/${TEST_ENV}-storageState.json`). Do NOT add any auth logic to the generated test.

**Non-DevRev URLs**: Do NOT use or ask about storage state.

[Test Generator Agent > Step 3: Gap Filling with playwright-cli]

**Skip this step if `--no-browser` flag is set.** Instead, insert `// TODO: Add URL assertion after navigation` and `// TODO: Add visibility assertion for key elements` comments at appropriate locations.

For each navigation-triggering action in the codegen script:

1. Load storage state if available: `playwright-cli state-load .playwright-cli/storageState-<name>.json`
2. Navigate to the pre-action page: `playwright-cli goto <url>`
3. Take snapshot: `playwright-cli snapshot` — get accessibility tree with element refs
4. Map raw codegen selectors to snapshot element refs
5. Execute the action: `playwright-cli click <ref>` — this auto-generates semantic Playwright code
6. Take post-action snapshot: `playwright-cli snapshot` — capture new URL and visible elements
7. Record a navigation map entry: `{ action, preURL, postURL, postVisibleElements, semanticLocator }`

**Key insight**: Every `playwright-cli` action auto-generates semantic Playwright TypeScript code (e.g., `await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click()`). Capture these as the canonical locators rather than heuristic guessing.

Close the browser when done: `playwright-cli close`

[Test Generator Agent > Step 4: POM Architecture (the critical step)]

Codegen puts everything inline. Sentinel requires locators and interactions to live in page/component/section classes. This step encapsulates raw codegen locators into the correct POM layer.

[Test Generator Agent > Step 4: POM Architecture (the critical step) > 4a. Scan Existing POM Classes]

Read the fixture files to discover what already exists:

1. Read `src/fixtures/page.fixtures.ts` and `src/fixtures/component.fixture.ts` to get the full list of available fixtures
2. For each fixture that might be relevant to the codegen actions, read the actual class file to discover its **locators and methods** (not just the fixture name)
3. Read `src/utils/page.utils.ts` for existing navigation helpers (e.g., `navigateToUpdatesPage()`)
4. Check `src/utils/` for other relevant utilities

[Test Generator Agent > Step 4: POM Architecture (the critical step) > 4b. Map Codegen Actions to Existing Methods]

For each codegen action, check if an existing fixture method already covers it:

- Navigation to a page → existing `goTo*()` or `navigateTo*()` methods
- Clicking a sidebar item → `navigationSideBar.goToWork*Vista()`, etc.
- Filling a form → existing `fill*()` or `create*()` methods on a page/component
- Asserting visibility → existing `assert*Displayed()` or `assert*Populated()` methods

**If a match exists**: use the fixture method instead of inline locators.

metadata.section: 4b. Map Codegen Actions to Existing Methods
headingHierarchy: 4b. Map Codegen Actions to Existing Methods

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 4: POM Architecture (the critical step) > 4c. Identify Locators That Need New POM Homes]

For each remaining inline locator (no existing method match), determine where it belongs using the decision table from `01-architecture.md`:

| Question                                                    | Answer → File Type |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ |
| Reusable across multiple pages? (modals, dialogs, sidebars) | **Component**      |
| Unique to a single page's logic?                            | **Page**           |
| Sub-area of a page? (tabs, panels within a parent)          | **Section**        |

Group related locators together — e.g., all "Create view" dialog locators go into one component.

metadata.section: 4c. Identify Locators That Need New POM Homes
headingHierarchy: 4c. Identify Locators That Need New POM Homes

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 4: POM Architecture (the critical step) > 4d. Create or Update POM Classes]

For each group identified in 4c:

1. **Check if a class already exists** (Glob for `*<name>*.component.ts`, `*<name>*.page.ts`, etc.)
2. **If exists**: add new locators to its `locator` getter and new methods following existing patterns in the class
3. **If new**: create the class following the templates in `05-page-objects.md`:
   - Locators in `public get locator()` getter, grouped by element type
   - Element-prefixed names per `02-naming-conventions.md` (`buttonSubmit`, `textBoxTitle`, `dialogCreateView`)
   - Action methods: `verb+Target` pattern (e.g., `fillTitle()`, `selectObjectType()`, `create()`)
   - Assertion methods: `assert*` pattern (e.g., `assertVisible()`, `assertHidden()`)
   - Compound convenience methods where appropriate (e.g., `createView({ title, objectType })`)

4. **Register new fixtures**: If a new component or page class was created, add it to the appropriate fixture file (`component.fixture.ts` or `page.fixtures.ts`) following the existing pattern.

[Test Generator Agent > Step 4: POM Architecture (the critical step) > 4e. Add Methods to Existing Classes When Needed]

If the codegen reveals an action that logically belongs on an existing class but isn't there yet:

- A new navigation trigger → add a method to `NavigationSideBar` (e.g., `openWorkCreateViewDialog()`)
- A new assertion on a vista → add a method to `WorksVista` (e.g., `assertCustomVistaDisplayed()`)
- A new form flow on a page → add method to the relevant page class

Follow the naming and structure patterns already in that class.

metadata.section: 4e. Add Methods to Existing Classes When Needed
headingHierarchy: 4e. Add Methods to Existing Classes When Needed

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format]

Apply the rules read at the start. Key codegen-specific transformations:

metadata.section: Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format
headingHierarchy: Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format > 5a. Structure (per `06-test-structure.md`)]

- Replace `import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'` with `import { test, expect } from '@fixtures/index.fixtures'`
- Wrap in `test.describe('{{feature}}', { tag: ['{{tags}}'] }, () => { ... })`
- Descriptive test name: `'should {{behavior}}'`
- AAA comments: `// Arrange`, `// Act`, `// Assert`

headingHierarchy: Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format > 5b. Use Fixtures and POM Methods]

- Destructure all relevant fixtures in the test signature: `async ({ page, navigationSideBar, createViewDialog, worksVista })`
- Replace inline codegen actions with fixture method calls from Step 4
- The test file should contain **minimal to no raw locators** — locators live in POM classes

headingHierarchy: Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format > 5c. Codegen-Specific Locator Fixes]

For any locators that remain inline (temporary or simple one-off assertions), apply the priority from `03-locators.md`:

| Codegen Pattern                        | Sentinel Replacement                                                 |
| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `page.locator('[data-drid="x"]')`      | `page.getByTestId('x')`                                              |
| `page.locator('button:has-text("X")')` | `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'X' })`                            |
| `page.locator('input[name="x"]')`      | `page.getByLabel('X')` or `page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'X' })` |
| `page.locator('#id')` / `.class`       | Use gap-filling snapshot data, or `getByTestId`/`getByRole`          |
| `page.click(selector)`                 | `page.locator(selector).click()` then apply locator rules            |
| `page.fill(selector, val)`             | `page.locator(selector).fill(val)` then apply locator rules          |

If gap-filling was done (Step 3), prefer `playwright-cli`'s auto-generated locators directly.

headingHierarchy: Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format > 5d. Aria Snapshot Handling]

Codegen often produces `toMatchAriaSnapshot()` assertions. These are **verbose, brittle, and contain env-specific data** (URLs, org names, dynamic IDs). Convert them:

1. **Extract the key elements** from the aria snapshot — identify what the snapshot is actually verifying (e.g., a dialog has certain fields, a table has certain columns, a tab is selected)
2. **Replace with targeted assertions** using auto-retrying matchers:
   - Dialog visible: `await expect(dialog).toBeVisible()`
   - Tab selected: `await expect(page.getByRole('tab', { name: 'X', selected: true })).toBeVisible()`
   - Table columns: `await expect(treegrid.getByRole('columnheader', { name: 'Title' })).toBeVisible()`
   - Data populated: use existing component methods like `worksVista.assertIssuesPopulated()`
3. **Discard the bulk** — the repetitive row data, encoded URLs, and full DOM trees are not useful assertions

headingHierarchy: Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format > 5e. Navigation Assertions (per `04-assertions.md`)]

- After every `page.goto()`: add `await expect(page).toHaveURL(/pattern/)`
- After every navigation-triggering click: add URL assertion from gap-filling data
- After page load: add visibility assertion for 1-3 key elements

headingHierarchy: Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format > 5f. Environment Agnosticism]

- Strip domain from URLs — use relative paths (rely on `baseURL` from config)
- Remove any hardcoded credentials or environment-specific values
- Use `@utils/env.utils` patterns for any required env vars

headingHierarchy: Step 5: Transform to Sentinel Test Format

content:
[Test Generator Agent > Step 6: Output, Verify, Report]

1. **Write POM files first**: any new or updated component/page/section classes and fixture registrations
2. **Write test file**: determine output path from `--output` or infer from feature name as `tests/<kebab-case-feature>/<camelCaseFeature>.test.ts`
3. **Type check**: run `npx tsc --noEmit` — if errors, fix them and re-check
4. **Offer to run**: ask user if they want to execute the test (`npx playwright test <file> --headed`)
5. **Report** what was done:
   - POM classes created or updated (with file paths)
   - Fixture registrations added
   - Existing methods reused vs new methods created
   - Locator transformations applied
   - Aria snapshots converted
   - Any `// TODO` comments that need manual attention

[Test Generator Agent > Verification Checklist]

Before outputting, verify every item. Rules references in parentheses:

**Test file** (`06-test-structure.md`):

- [ ] Imports from `@fixtures/index.fixtures` (not `@playwright/test`)
- [ ] `test.describe` with tags array
- [ ] Descriptive test name starting with "should"
- [ ] AAA pattern with `// Arrange`, `// Act`, `// Assert` comments
- [ ] Each test is independent (explicit state setup)

**POM architecture** (`01-architecture.md`, `05-page-objects.md`):

- [ ] No raw locators in test file — locators live in page/component/section classes
- [ ] New POM classes follow the correct template (locator getter, typed methods)
- [ ] New classes registered as fixtures in the appropriate fixture file
- [ ] Existing fixture methods reused where available

**Locators** (`03-locators.md`, `02-naming-conventions.md`):

- [ ] Priority hierarchy: `getByRole()`/`getByTestId()` > `getByLabel()` > `getByText()` > `locator()`
- [ ] Element-prefixed names in POM classes (`buttonSubmit`, `textBoxEmail`)
- [ ] Method names follow `assert*` / `verb+Target` / `get*` patterns

**Assertions & banned methods** (`04-assertions.md`, root `CLAUDE.md`):

- [ ] No banned methods (`waitForTimeout`, `waitForNavigation`, `$()`, `$$()`, `waitForSelector`, `page.click()`, `page.fill()`, `networkidle`)
- [ ] URL assertion after every navigation action
- [ ] Visibility assertion for key elements after page loads
- [ ] Auto-retrying assertions for UI state; non-retrying for data
- [ ] No `toMatchAriaSnapshot()` — converted to targeted assertions

**General**:

- [ ] All Playwright actions are awaited
- [ ] Relative URLs only (no hardcoded domains)
- [ ] No hardcoded credentials or environment-specific values
- [ ] Passes `npx tsc --noEmit`

---
paths:
  - src/pages/**
  - src/components/**
  - src/fixtures/**
---

[Architecture Principles > Hybrid POM + Component Approach]

The framework uses a three-tier structure that balances readability, maintainability, and reusability.

[Architecture Principles > Hybrid POM + Component Approach > Three-Tier Structure > 1. Pages (`*.page.ts`)]

**Navigable screens accessible directly from sidebar/URL**

- Represent standalone navigable screens
- Contains page-specific locators and business logic
- Can compose sections and components
- **Examples:** `settings.page.ts`, `updates.page.ts`, `userProfile.page.ts`

[Architecture Principles > Hybrid POM + Component Approach > Three-Tier Structure > 2. Sections (`*.section.ts`)]

**Sub-areas within a page (not standalone)**

- Belong to a specific page, not shared across pages
- Represent tabs, panels, or sections within a parent page
- **Example:** `preferences.section.ts` inside `agent/` folder

[Architecture Principles > Hybrid POM + Component Approach > Three-Tier Structure > 3. Components (`*.component.ts`)]

**Reusable UI elements across multiple pages**

- Used by multiple pages/sections
- Contains reusable interaction patterns
- **Examples:** `navigationSideBar.component.ts`, `sideDrawer.component.ts`, `globalSearchModal.component.ts`

[Architecture Principles > Hybrid POM + Component Approach > Three-Tier Structure > 4. Widgets (`widgets/*.component.ts`)]

**Specialized dashboard/chart components**

- Located in `src/components/dashboards/widgets/`
- Each widget type has its own component class
- Widgets are instantiated via a parent Dashboard component
- **Examples:** `metric.component.ts`, `columnChart.component.ts`, `table.component.ts`

[Architecture Principles > Hybrid POM + Component Approach > Decision Rule]

**When creating new files, ask:**

| Question                                 | Answer → File Type                |
| ---------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| Reusable across multiple pages?          | **Component**                     |
| Unique to a single page's logic?         | **Page**                          |
| Sub-area of a page?                      | **Section**                       |
| Dashboard widget (chart, table, metric)? | **Widget** (in `widgets/` folder) |

[Architecture Principles > Fixture Pattern]

**ALWAYS use fixtures for Pages and Components**

[Architecture Principles > Fixture Pattern > Fixture Structure]

```
src/fixtures/
├── page.fixtures.ts        # Page object fixtures
├── component.fixture.ts    # Component fixtures
└── index.fixtures.ts       # Merged exports (use this in tests)
```

**Important:** Always import from `@fixtures/index.fixtures` in test files.

[Architecture Principles > Folder Structure]

```
src/
├── components/              # Reusable components (common across multiple pages)
│   ├── navigationSideBar.component.ts
│   ├── sideDrawer.component.ts
│   └── dashboards/
│       └── widgets/         # Dashboard widget components
│           ├── metric.component.ts
│           ├── columnChart.component.ts
│           └── table.component.ts
│
├── pages/                   # Page-specific classes
│   ├── updates/
│   │   └── updates.page.ts
│   ├── settings/
│   │   ├── settings.page.ts
│   │   ├── general.page.ts
│   │   └── agent/
│   │       ├── agent.page.ts
│   │       ├── preferences.section.ts  # Section within agent page
│   │       └── analytics.section.ts
│   └── ...
│
├── fixtures/                # Playwright fixtures
│   ├── page.fixtures.ts
│   ├── component.fixture.ts
│   └── index.fixtures.ts
│
├── utils/                   # Helpers, constants, API clients
│
└── tests/                   # Test files
    ├── user-journeys-web/
    └── ...
```

[Architecture Principles > Best Practices]

✅ **DO:**

- Use element-prefixed locator names (`buttonSubmit`, `textBoxEmail`)
- Keep locators in the closest relevant layer
- Reference components instead of duplicating locators
- Keep business flows in pages, not components

❌ **DON'T:**

- Duplicate component locators inside pages
- Put business flows in components
- Over-engineer by splitting tiny elements into separate files unless reused

---

**See also:**

- `docs/best-practices/hybrid-pom-component-approach.md` - Detailed architecture guide
- `docs/best-practices/naming-conventions.md` - File naming standards

---
paths:
  - src/**/*.ts
  - tests/**/*.ts
---

[Naming Conventions (Detailed Method & Locator Patterns)]

General naming tables (files, folders, code) are in root `CLAUDE.md`. This rule covers detailed method and locator naming patterns.

[Naming Conventions (Detailed Method & Locator Patterns) > Method Naming Patterns > Assertion Methods (`assert*`)]

Follow the pattern: `assert[Thing][State]` or `assert[Thing]Is[State]`

```typescript
// ✅ GOOD - Clear, self-documenting assertion names
async assertMenuIsDisplayed(): Promise<void>
async assertDashboardHeaderDisplayed(): Promise<void>
async assertDataLabelsVisible(): Promise<void>
async assertTooltipVisible(): Promise<void>
async assertValueIsNumeric(): Promise<void>
async assertLegendItemEnabled(legendName: string): Promise<void>
async assertLegendItemDisabled(legendName: string): Promise<void>
async assertColumnValuesEqual(options: { columnName: string; expectedValue: string }): Promise<void>

// ❌ BAD - Implementation details, unclear, or index-based
async assertSeriesVisible(seriesIndex: number)  // Uses index instead of name
async assertLegendSeriesVisible(legendName)     // "Series" is implementation detail
async checkVisible()                            // Too vague
async verify()                                  // Not descriptive
```

**Key Principles for Assertion Methods:**

1. **Use user-facing terminology** - Prefer "LegendItem" over "Series" (users see legend items)
2. **Match UI state names** - Use "Enabled/Disabled" if that's what the data attribute uses
3. **Avoid index-based parameters** - Use descriptive names that are environment-agnostic
4. **Be self-documenting** - Method name should explain what it asserts without reading implementation

[Naming Conventions (Detailed Method & Locator Patterns) > Method Naming Patterns > Action Methods]

Follow the pattern: `[verb][Target]` or `[verb][Target]For[Purpose]`

```typescript
// ✅ GOOD - Clear action methods
async clickValueForDrillthrough(): Promise<void>
async clickFirstDataPointForDrillthrough(): Promise<void>
async toggleLegendItem(legendName: string): Promise<void>
async openMenu(): Promise<void>
async showColumn(columnName: string): Promise<void>
async hideColumn(columnName: string): Promise<void>
async selectGroupOption(option: string): Promise<void>
async resetGrouping(): Promise<void>

// ❌ BAD - Unclear or too generic
async click()                    // What are we clicking?
async toggle()                   // Toggle what?
async doAction()                 // Not descriptive
```

[Naming Conventions (Detailed Method & Locator Patterns) > Method Naming Patterns > Getter Methods]

Follow the pattern: `get[Thing]` or `get[Thing]By[Attribute]`

```typescript
// ✅ GOOD - Clear getter methods
getColumnHeader(name: string | RegExp): Locator
getLegendItem(name: string): Locator
getAxisLabel(text: string | RegExp): Locator
getGroupItem(name: string): Locator
getCustomizeColumnOption(columnName: string): Locator

// ❌ BAD - Unclear or inconsistent
getItem()           // What kind of item?
findElement()       // Too generic
```

---
paths:
  - src/pages/**
  - src/components/**
  - tests/**/*.test.ts
---

[Playwright Locator Hierarchy > Priority Order]

**ALWAYS follow this priority order:**

[Playwright Locator Hierarchy > Priority Order > 3. `getByText()`]

**For static text content**

```typescript
await page.getByText('Welcome to our app');
await page.getByText(/Loading.../); // Regex support
```

⚠️ Avoid for buttons/links; prefer `getByRole()`

[Playwright Locator Hierarchy > Priority Order > 4. `locator()` - LAST RESORT]

**CSS/XPath when no other option works**

```typescript
await page.locator('#login-form').click(); // Only when necessary
```

[Playwright Locator Hierarchy > Advanced Patterns > Chaining Locators]

```typescript
await page.locator('#user-list').getByRole('listitem').filter({ hasText: 'John Doe' }).getByRole('button', { name: 'Edit' }).click();
```

[Playwright Locator Hierarchy > Advanced Patterns > Filtering]

```typescript
await page.getByRole('listitem').filter({ hasText: 'Product 2' }).getByRole('button').click();
```

[Playwright Locator Hierarchy > Highcharts/SVG Chart Locators]

When working with Highcharts or SVG-based charts, use these patterns:

[Playwright Locator Hierarchy > Chart Interaction Best Practices]

1. **Use CSS class selectors** for Highcharts elements
   - Examples: `.highcharts-series-0`, `.highcharts-point`

2. **Use `data-drid` attributes** for legend items and interactive elements
   - Provides stable, test-friendly selectors

3. **Avoid clicking on data labels with specific values**
   - Values change between environments
   - Use `.first()` or `.nth()` instead

4. **Use `.first()` or `.nth()` for multiple matches**
   - Better than relying on specific data values

[Playwright Locator Hierarchy > Element-Prefixed Naming]

**Always prefix locator names with the element type:**

| Element Type | Prefix     | Example                               |
| ------------ | ---------- | ------------------------------------- |
| Button       | `button`   | `buttonSubmit`, `buttonCancel`        |
| Text input   | `textBox`  | `textBoxEmail`, `textBoxPassword`     |
| Link         | `link`     | `linkForgotPassword`, `linkHome`      |
| Dropdown     | `dropDown` | `dropDownCountry`, `dropDownRole`     |
| Checkbox     | `checkBox` | `checkBoxRememberMe`, `checkBoxAgree` |
| Radio button | `radio`    | `radioGender`, `radioPaymentMethod`   |

---
paths:
  - tests/**/*.test.ts
  - src/pages/**
  - src/components/**
---

[Assertion Best Practices > Configuration]

Global timeout settings (from `playwright.config.ts`):

- **Global assertion timeout:** 15s
- **Test timeout:** 180s
- **Action timeout:** 60s
- **Navigation timeout:** 100s

[Assertion Best Practices > Quick Reference]

| Assertion Type | Method                                                   | Use Case              |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| Visibility     | `await expect(locator).toBeVisible()`                    | Element is visible    |
| Hidden         | `await expect(locator).toBeHidden()`                     | Element is hidden     |
| Text           | `await expect(locator).toHaveText('text')`               | Element contains text |
| Value          | `await expect(locator).toHaveValue('value')`             | Input has value       |
| Count          | `await expect(locator).toHaveCount(5)`                   | Number of elements    |
| URL            | `await expect(page).toHaveURL(/pattern/)`                | Page URL matches      |
| Enabled        | `await expect(locator).toBeEnabled()`                    | Element is enabled    |
| Disabled       | `await expect(locator).toBeDisabled()`                   | Element is disabled   |
| Attribute      | `await expect(locator).toHaveAttribute('name', 'value')` | Element has attribute |

[Assertion Best Practices > Best Practices]

✅ **DO:**

- Use auto-retrying assertions for UI state
- Use non-retrying assertions for data comparisons
- Set custom timeouts for slow operations
- Use `.toBeHidden()` or `.toHaveCount(0)` for absence checks

❌ **DON'T:**

- Use `isVisible()` in conditionals (use `expect().toBeVisible()` instead)
- Mix auto-retrying and non-retrying assertions incorrectly
- Set unnecessarily long timeouts globally

---

**See also:**

- `docs/best-practices/playwright-assertions-auto-retrying-vs-non-retrying.md` - Detailed assertion guide
- `docs/best-practices/methods-to-avoid-playwright.md` - Deprecated assertion patterns

---
paths:
  - src/pages/**
  - src/components/**
---

[Page Object Structure > Best Practices > Locator Organization]

✅ **DO:**

- Group locators by element type (buttons, inputs, links, etc.)
- Use element-prefixed naming (`buttonSubmit`, `textBoxEmail`)
- Use getter method pattern (`public get locator()`)
- Return locators as an object for easy access

❌ **DON'T:**

- Store locators as class properties (they should be computed)
- Mix locators with business logic in the same section
- Use generic names without element prefix

[Page Object Structure > Best Practices > Business Logic Methods]

✅ **DO:**

- Create action-oriented methods (`fillUserProfile`, `submitForm`)
- Use descriptive method names that explain intent
- Keep methods focused on single actions
- Use async/await for all Playwright actions

❌ **DON'T:**

- Put complex business logic in components (belongs in pages)
- Create methods that do too many things
- Use vague method names (`doAction`, `process`)

[Page Object Structure > Best Practices > Navigation Methods]

✅ **DO:**

- Add `open()` or `goto()` methods for pages
- Use relative URLs from baseURL
- Wait for page-specific elements after navigation

```typescript
async open(): Promise<void> {
	await this.page.goto('/settings/agent');
	await expect(this.locator.tabPreferences).toBeVisible();
}
```

---

**See also:**

- `01-architecture.md` - When to use Page vs Section vs Component
- `02-naming-conventions.md` - Naming standards for classes and methods
- `03-locators.md` - Locator hierarchy and patterns

---
paths:
  - tests/**/*.test.ts
---

[Test Structure > Test Consolidation & Reliability > Parallel Test Considerations]

- Tests run in parallel by default - never rely on execution order
- Use `test.describe.serial` only when absolutely necessary
- Prefer resetting state within each test over shared state

metadata.section: ❌ NEVER Weaken Tests to Make Them Pass
headingHierarchy: ❌ NEVER Weaken Tests to Make Them Pass

metadata.section: ✅ DO Investigate and Fix Root Cause
headingHierarchy: ✅ DO Investigate and Fix Root Cause

content:
[Test Structure > Test Reliability Principles > Key Reliability Principles]

1. **If it works manually, it should be automatable** - Don't give up
2. **Don't remove or weaken assertions** - Fix the underlying issue
3. **Investigate failures thoroughly** - Read trace files, check HTML structure
4. **Use environment-agnostic locators** - Avoid hardcoded values
5. **Reset state explicitly** - Don't assume state from previous tests
6. **Prefer robust over brittle** - CSS classes over text content or indices

[Test Structure > When a Test Fails]

1. **Examine trace files** - `npx playwright show-trace trace.zip`
2. **Check the HTML structure** - Understand the actual DOM
3. **Verify manually** - Can you perform the action in the browser?
4. **Find alternative locators** - Use Playwright Inspector or DevTools
5. **Update the locator/logic** - Make it robust, not just passing

paths:
  - playwright.config.ts
  - playwright.computer.config.ts
  - eslint.config.mts
  - tsconfig.json

---

[Configuration Standards > Project Configuration (`playwright.config.ts`) > Key Settings]

| Setting           | Value                                 | Description                   |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| `testIdAttribute` | `data-drid`                           | Custom attribute for test IDs |
| `baseURL`         | `process.env.BASE_URL`                | Base URL from environment     |
| `timeout`         | `180000` (180s)                       | Test timeout                  |
| `expect.timeout`  | `15000` (15s)                         | Assertion timeout             |
| `trace`           | `retain-on-failure`                   | Traces on failure only        |
| `video`           | CI: `retain-on-failure`, local: `off` | Video on failures (CI only)   |
| `headless`        | `true`                                | Headless by default           |

[Configuration Standards > Environment Variables > Environment File Structure]

```
.env                    # Local environment variables (gitignored)
```

Common environment variables:

- `BASE_URL` - Application base URL
- `ORG_ID` - Organization ID
- `PAT` - Personal Access Token
- `ENVIRONMENT` - Current environment (dev/staging/prod)

[Configuration Standards > Test ID Attribute]

The project uses a **custom test ID attribute**: `data-drid`

[Configuration Standards > Test ID Attribute > When to Use `data-drid`]

Use `data-drid` when:

- User-facing attributes (role, label, text) are unreliable
- Element has no accessible role
- Text content changes frequently
- Need a stable, test-specific identifier

[Configuration Standards > Trace and Debug Configuration > Trace Files]

- Enabled on failure (`trace: 'retain-on-failure'`)
- Captured when a test fails
- Viewable with: `npx playwright show-trace trace.zip`

[Configuration Standards > Trace and Debug Configuration > Video Recording]

- In CI: `video: 'retain-on-failure'`
- Locally: `video: 'off'` (disabled)
- Stored in `test-results/` directory

[Configuration Standards > Project Structure Settings > Important Notes]

- **Parallel Execution:** Tests run in parallel by default
- **Retries in CI:** 2 retries in CI, 1 locally
- **Workers:** 3 workers in CI, 50% locally
- **Reports:** HTML report generated in `playwright-report/`

[Configuration Standards > Best Practices]

✅ **DO:**

- Use environment variables for configuration
- Use `@utils/env.utils` for env access
- Leverage the custom `data-drid` attribute
- Keep timeouts reasonable
- Use trace files for debugging

❌ **DON'T:**

- Hardcode environment-specific values
- Commit `.env` file to git
- Set unnecessarily long global timeouts
- Disable traces in CI

---

**See also:**

- `playwright.config.ts` - Full configuration file
- `src/utils/env.utils.ts` - Environment utilities
- `03-locators.md` - Using `getByTestId()` with `data-drid`

name: git-workflow
description: Guide and automate Git workflow following the Sentinel project's Git Flow conventions including branch naming, commit messages, and PR creation.
allowed-tools: Bash(git *), Bash(gh *)
disable-model-invocation: true
argument-hint: '[branch|commit|pr]'
---

[Git Workflow]

Guide and automate Git workflow for the Sentinel project.

[Git Workflow > Usage]

```
/git-workflow [branch|commit|pr]
```

[Git Workflow > Branch Naming]

| Type    | Prefix     | Example                        |
| ------- | ---------- | ------------------------------ |
| Feature | `feature/` | `feature/add-login-tests`      |
| Bug Fix | `fix/`     | `fix/resolve-flaky-test`       |
| Hotfix  | `hotfix/`  | `hotfix/critical-selector-fix` |
| Chore   | `chore/`   | `chore/update-dependencies`    |

Always branch from `develop`:

```bash
git checkout develop && git pull origin develop
git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
```

[Git Workflow > Commit Messages]

Conventional commit format:

```
<type>: <description>
```

| Type       | Usage                     |
| ---------- | ------------------------- |
| `feat`     | New feature or test       |
| `fix`      | Bug fix                   |
| `refactor` | Code refactoring          |
| `test`     | Adding or modifying tests |
| `chore`    | Maintenance tasks         |
| `docs`     | Documentation changes     |
| `style`    | Code style changes        |
| `perf`     | Performance improvements  |

Examples:

```bash
git commit -m "feat: add authentication tests for login page"
git commit -m "fix: resolve flaky test in dashboard component"
git commit -m "refactor: improve locator structure in settings page"
```

[Git Workflow > Pull Request Process]

1. Target branch: `develop`
2. Ensure all checks pass: ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript, Playwright tests
3. Create PR using `gh` CLI:

```bash
gh pr create --base develop --title "feat: add login tests" --body "## Summary
- Added login page E2E tests

[Git Workflow > Test plan]

- [ ] Smoke tests pass"
```

[Git Workflow > Pre-commit Hooks (automatic)]

- **ESLint** - lints staged TypeScript files
- **Prettier** - formats staged files
- **TypeScript** - `tsc --noEmit` type checking

[Git Workflow > Pre-push Hooks (automatic)]

- **Playwright tests** - runs tests for changed files against `origin/develop`

If hooks fail, fix the issue rather than bypassing with `--no-verify`.

---
name: plan
description: Create a detailed implementation plan before writing code. Scans existing codebase for reusable page objects and components, breaks work into phases, and waits for your approval before proceeding.
allowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash(git diff *), Bash(git log *)
disable-model-invocation: true
argument-hint: '<description of what to build or test>'
context: fork
agent: planner
---

[Implementation Planner]

Create a detailed, phased implementation plan before writing any code.

[Implementation Planner > Usage]

```
/plan <description of what to build or test>
```

[Implementation Planner > Examples]

```
/plan E2E tests for the billing settings page
/plan Refactor navigation sidebar page object to support new menu structure
/plan Add dashboard widget component for pie charts
/plan Test suite for the create-issue workflow with feature toggles
```

[Implementation Planner > What It Does]

1. **Analyzes** your request and restates requirements clearly
2. **Scans** the codebase for existing page objects, components, and utilities to reuse
3. **Creates** a phased plan with specific file paths, actions, and dependencies
4. **Identifies** risks (flaky selectors, auth needs, environment dependencies)
5. **Waits** for your explicit approval before any code is written

[Implementation Planner > Why Plan First]

- Avoids creating duplicate page objects or components that already exist
- Ensures correct POM architecture (Page vs Section vs Component) from the start
- Catches dependency issues early (missing fixtures, utils)
- Gives you a chance to adjust scope before implementation begins

[Request Mocking]

Intercept, mock, modify, and block network requests.

[Request Mocking > CLI Route Commands]

```bash

[Mock with custom status]

playwright-cli route "**/*.jpg" --status=404

[Mock with JSON body]

playwright-cli route "**/api/users" --body='[{"id":1,"name":"Alice"}]' --content-type=application/json

[Mock with custom headers]

playwright-cli route "**/api/data" --body='{"ok":true}' --header="X-Custom: value"

[Remove headers from requests]

playwright-cli route "**/*" --remove-header=cookie,authorization

[List active routes]

playwright-cli route-list

[Remove a route or all routes]

playwright-cli unroute "**/*.jpg"
playwright-cli unroute
```

[Already logged in! > Save and Restore Roundtrip]

```bash

[Set up authentication state]

playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli eval "() => { document.cookie = 'session=abc123'; localStorage.setItem('user', 'john'); }"

[Save state to file]

playwright-cli state-save my-session.json

[Restore state]

playwright-cli state-load my-session.json
playwright-cli open https://example.com

[Cookies and localStorage are restored!]

```

[Cookies and localStorage are restored! > Security Notes]

- Never commit storage state files containing auth tokens
- Add `*.auth-state.json` to `.gitignore`
- Delete state files after automation completes
- Use environment variables for sensitive data
- By default, sessions run in-memory mode which is safer for sensitive operations

[Tracing]

Capture detailed execution traces for debugging and analysis. Traces include DOM snapshots, screenshots, network activity, and console logs.

[Tracing > Basic Usage]

```bash

[Start trace recording]

playwright-cli tracing-start

[Perform actions]

playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli click e1
playwright-cli fill e2 "test"

[Stop trace recording]

playwright-cli tracing-stop
```

[Stop trace recording > Trace Output Files]

When you start tracing, Playwright creates a `traces/` directory with several files:

[Stop trace recording > Trace Output Files > `trace-{timestamp}.trace`]

**Action log** - The main trace file containing:

- Every action performed (clicks, fills, navigations)
- DOM snapshots before and after each action
- Screenshots at each step
- Timing information
- Console messages
- Source locations

[Stop trace recording > Trace Output Files > `trace-{timestamp}.network`]

**Network log** - Complete network activity:

- All HTTP requests and responses
- Request headers and bodies
- Response headers and bodies
- Timing (DNS, connect, TLS, TTFB, download)
- Resource sizes
- Failed requests and errors

[Stop trace recording > Trace Output Files > `resources/`]

**Resources directory** - Cached resources:

- Images, fonts, stylesheets, scripts
- Response bodies for replay
- Assets needed to reconstruct page state

[Stop trace recording > What Traces Capture]

| Category        | Details                                            |
| --------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| **Actions**     | Clicks, fills, hovers, keyboard input, navigations |
| **DOM**         | Full DOM snapshot before/after each action         |
| **Screenshots** | Visual state at each step                          |
| **Network**     | All requests, responses, headers, bodies, timing   |
| **Console**     | All console.log, warn, error messages              |
| **Timing**      | Precise timing for each operation                  |

[Stop trace recording > Use Cases > Debugging Failed Actions]

```bash
playwright-cli tracing-start
playwright-cli open https://app.example.com

[This click fails - why?]

playwright-cli click e5

playwright-cli tracing-stop

[Open trace to see DOM state when click was attempted]

```

---
name: review-code
description: Run a comprehensive code quality review against Sentinel framework standards. Checks architecture, naming, Playwright best practices, TypeScript, and test quality.
allowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash(git diff *), Bash(npx eslint *), Bash(npx tsc *)
disable-model-invocation: true
argument-hint: '[--files=<file1,file2>] [--all]'
context: fork
agent: code-reviewer
---

[Code Quality Review]

Run a comprehensive quality review on changed files against Sentinel framework standards.

[Code Quality Review > Changed Files > Staged (uncommitted)]

!`git diff --cached --name-only`

[Code Quality Review > Changed Files > Committed on branch (vs develop)]

!`git diff --name-only origin/develop...HEAD`

[Code Quality Review > Diff > Staged (uncommitted)]

!`git diff --cached`

[Code Quality Review > Diff > Committed on branch (vs develop)]

!`git diff origin/develop...HEAD`

[Code Quality Review > Usage]

```
/review-code [--files=<file1,file2>] [--all]
```

[Code Quality Review > Process]

1. **Determine scope**: If `$ARGUMENTS` specifies `--files=`, review ONLY those files. If `--all` or no arguments, review all changed files listed above.
2. **Run automated checks**: `npx eslint` on in-scope files, `npx tsc --noEmit` project-wide (it cannot be scoped to individual files)
3. **Review the diff** focusing on changed lines. Read full files only when surrounding context is needed.
4. **Separate findings** — issues introduced by the diff are FAIL/WARN; pre-existing issues visible from context are NIT in a separate section.
5. **Check documentation consistency** (see Documentation Consistency checklist below) when changes touch docs, rules, or config files.
6. **Report** PASS/WARN/FAIL per category with `file:line` references

[Code Quality Review > Review Checklist > Clean Code & Design Principles]

- [ ] **SRP** - Each class/file has one responsibility (page = one screen, component = one reusable UI element, section = one sub-area)
- [ ] **SOC** - Locators, business logic, and assertions are clearly separated; components don't contain business logic
- [ ] **DRY** (page objects/components) - No duplicated locators or methods across files; shared logic extracted to components
- [ ] **DAMP** (tests) - Tests prioritize readability over DRY; descriptive names, explicit setup, clear intent even if slightly repetitive

[Code Quality Review > Review Checklist > Architecture & Organization]

- [ ] Follows Hybrid POM + Component architecture (Pages/Sections/Components/Widgets)
- [ ] Files in correct directories (`src/pages/`, `src/components/`, `tests/`)
- [ ] Correct file suffix (`.page.ts`, `.section.ts`, `.component.ts`, `.test.ts`)
- [ ] No duplicate logic across files

[Code Quality Review > Review Checklist > Naming Conventions]

- [ ] Folders: `kebab-case`
- [ ] Files: `camelCase` + suffix
- [ ] Classes: `PascalCase`
- [ ] Variables/functions: `camelCase`
- [ ] Constants: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`
- [ ] Locators: element-prefixed (`buttonSubmit`, `textBoxEmail`)
- [ ] Methods: `assert*` / `verb+Target` / `get*` patterns

[Code Quality Review > Review Checklist > Playwright Best Practices]

- [ ] Locator priority: `getByRole()` / `getByTestId()` (co-first) > `getByLabel()` > `getByText()` > `locator()`
- [ ] No banned methods: `waitForTimeout`, `waitForNavigation`, `$()`, `$$()`, `waitForSelector`, `page.click()`, `page.fill()`, `networkidle`
- [ ] No `isVisible()`/`isEnabled()` in conditionals
- [ ] Auto-retrying assertions for UI elements
- [ ] Non-retrying assertions for data comparisons

[Code Quality Review > Review Checklist > TypeScript Standards]

- [ ] Explicit types on params and return values
- [ ] No `any` type usage
- [ ] All Playwright actions are awaited
- [ ] Comments only for non-obvious logic

[Code Quality Review > Review Checklist > Test Quality (for `.test.ts` files)]

- [ ] Imports from `@fixtures/index.fixtures`
- [ ] Descriptive test names with tags
- [ ] Arrange-Act-Assert pattern
- [ ] Tests are independent (explicit state setup)
- [ ] No hardcoded URLs or environment-specific values
- [ ] No weakened assertions or swallowed errors

[Code Quality Review > Review Checklist > Documentation Consistency (when changes touch docs/rules/config)]

- [ ] Timeout values in rules match `playwright.config.ts` and root `CLAUDE.md`

[Code Quality Review > Code Review Results > file.page.ts]

- WARN: Naming - `submit` should be `buttonSubmit` (line 15)
- FAIL: Playwright - uses `waitForTimeout()` (line 23)

[Code Quality Review > Code Review Results > Pre-existing Issues (low priority)]

[Code Quality Review > Code Review Results > Summary]

- PASS: N | WARN: N | FAIL: N | NIT: N

name: scaffold-component
description: Generate a new reusable Component class following the Sentinel framework's Hybrid POM architecture. Components are shared across multiple pages.
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep
disable-model-invocation: true
argument-hint: '<ComponentName> [--path=src/components/]'
---

[Scaffold Component]

Generate a new reusable Component class for the Sentinel Playwright framework.

[Scaffold Component > Usage]

```
/scaffold-component <ComponentName> [--path=src/components/]
```

[Scaffold Component > Process]

1. **Ask** for the component name and target directory if not provided
2. **Check** if the component already exists to avoid overwriting
3. **Generate** the component file using the template below
4. **Register** the component in `src/fixtures/component.fixture.ts`
5. **Verify** the fixture export in `src/fixtures/index.fixtures.ts`

[Scaffold Component > Key Rules]

- Components are **reusable across multiple pages** - if it's page-specific, use a Section instead
- **No business logic** in components - that belongs in Page objects
- File: `camelCase.component.ts` (e.g., `sideDrawer.component.ts`)
- Class: `PascalCase` (e.g., `SideDrawer`)
- Locators: element-prefixed (`buttonClose`, `linkSettings`)

[Scaffold Component > Post-Generation Checklist]

- [ ] Component file created in `src/components/`
- [ ] Class follows `PascalCase` naming
- [ ] Locators use element-prefixed naming
- [ ] No business logic in component (only UI interactions)
- [ ] Component registered as fixture in `src/fixtures/component.fixture.ts`
- [ ] Fixture exported from `src/fixtures/index.fixtures.ts`
- [ ] Return types specified on all methods

name: scaffold-test
description: Generate a new test file following the Sentinel framework's test structure with proper imports, AAA pattern, tags, and fixture usage.
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep
disable-model-invocation: true
argument-hint: '<testName> [--path=tests/<folder>/]'
---

[Scaffold Test]

Generate a new test file for the Sentinel Playwright framework.

[Scaffold Test > Usage]

```
/scaffold-test <testName> [--path=tests/<folder>/]
```

[Scaffold Test > Process]

1. **Ask** for the test name, target directory, and relevant page objects/components if not provided
2. **Check** if the test file already exists to avoid overwriting
3. **Look up** available fixtures in `src/fixtures/index.fixtures.ts`
4. **Generate** the test file using the template below

[Scaffold Test > Key Rules]

- Always import `test, expect` from `@fixtures/index.fixtures`
- Follow **Arrange-Act-Assert** pattern
- Use descriptive test names: `'should display error when email is invalid'`
- Add tags: `@smoke`, `@regression`, `@functional`
- Each test must be independent (explicitly set up required state)
- Use auto-retrying assertions for UI: `await expect(locator).toBeVisible()`
- Use non-retrying assertions for data: `expect(value).toBe(expected)`
- No hardcoded URLs or environment-specific values
- Use fixtures for page objects and components (don't instantiate manually)

[Scaffold Test > Post-Generation Checklist]

- [ ] Test file created in correct directory under `tests/`
- [ ] Imports from `@fixtures/index.fixtures`
- [ ] Descriptive test names with tags
- [ ] AAA pattern followed
- [ ] Tests are independent (don't rely on execution order)
- [ ] Uses environment-agnostic locators and assertions
- [ ] No deprecated methods (`waitForTimeout`, `$()`, etc.)

name: transform-codegen
description: Transform raw Playwright codegen scripts into production-quality Sentinel test files. Fills gaps (navigation waits, assertions, auth), replaces raw selectors with semantic locators, and applies Sentinel conventions.
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash(playwright-cli:*), Bash(npx tsc *), Bash(npx playwright test *)
disable-model-invocation: true
argument-hint: '<codegen-script-path-or-inline> [--output=tests/<folder>/testName.test.ts] [--feature=FeatureName] [--tags=@smoke] [--no-browser]'
context: fork
agent: test-generator
---

[Codegen-to-Sentinel Test Generator]

Transform raw `npx playwright codegen` output into production-quality Sentinel test files.

[Codegen-to-Sentinel Test Generator > Usage]

```
/transform-codegen <codegen-script-or-path> [--output=tests/<folder>/testName.test.ts] [--feature=FeatureName] [--tags=@smoke] [--no-browser]
```

[Codegen-to-Sentinel Test Generator > Options]

| Option         | Description                                                                           | Default                            |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `--output`     | Output file path under `tests/`                                                       | Inferred from `--feature`          |
| `--feature`    | Feature name for `test.describe` block                                                | Inferred from codegen actions      |
| `--tags`       | Test tags (`@smoke`, `@regression`, `@functional`, `@integration`, `@feature-toggle`) | `@regression`                      |
| `--no-browser` | Skip live browser gap-filling; insert `// TODO` comments instead                      | Browser session used when possible |

[Codegen-to-Sentinel Test Generator > What It Does]

Codegen scripts have significant gaps that this skill fills:

1. **No waits/assertions** after navigation-triggering actions - adds URL and visibility assertions
2. **No authentication** - uses framework's `login-setup` project (no auth logic in tests)
3. **Raw CSS selectors** - replaces with `getByRole()`/`getByTestId()` semantic locators
4. **No framework conventions** - adds fixtures, AAA pattern, tags, proper imports

[Codegen-to-Sentinel Test Generator > Process]

This skill delegates to the `test-generator` agent, which:

1. **Parses** the codegen script (categorizes each line: navigation, interaction, assertion)
2. **Authenticates** via `playwright-cli` for DevRev URLs (reuses or creates storage state)
3. **Fills gaps** by replaying navigation-triggering actions in a live browser to capture post-action state
4. **Encapsulates** locators into page/component/section classes per POM architecture
5. **Transforms** to Sentinel test format (fixtures, AAA, naming) with minimal inline locators
6. **Verifies** output with `npx tsc --noEmit` and reports all files created/updated

name: verify
description: Run pre-PR verification pipeline — TypeScript, ESLint, Prettier, and optional Playwright tests. Use before committing or creating PRs to ensure all quality gates pass.
allowed-tools: Bash(npx tsc *), Bash(npm run lint*), Bash(npm run format*), Bash(npx playwright test *), Bash(git *)
disable-model-invocation: true
argument-hint: '[quick|full|pre-pr] [--test=<test-file>]'
---

[Pre-PR Verification]

Run the Sentinel quality gate pipeline to verify code is ready for commit or PR.

[Pre-PR Verification > Usage]

```
/verify [quick|full|pre-pr] [--test=<test-file>]
```

[Pre-PR Verification > Modes]

| Mode     | Steps                           | When to Use          |
| -------- | ------------------------------- | -------------------- |
| `quick`  | TypeScript + ESLint only        | During development   |
| `full`   | TypeScript + ESLint + Prettier  | Before committing    |
| `pre-pr` | Full + changed Playwright tests | Before creating a PR |

Default mode is `full` when no argument is provided.

[Pre-PR Verification > Pipeline]

Execute each step in order. **Stop on critical failures** — type errors and lint errors must be fixed before proceeding.

[Pre-PR Verification > Pipeline > Step 1: TypeScript Check]

```bash
npx tsc --noEmit
```

If this fails, report all errors with `file:line` references and **STOP**. Type errors must be fixed before continuing to other checks.

[Pre-PR Verification > Pipeline > Step 2: ESLint Check]

```bash
npm run lint
```

Report errors and warnings separately. Errors are blockers; warnings should be reviewed.

If there are auto-fixable issues, suggest: `npm run lint:fix`

[Pre-PR Verification > Pipeline > Step 3: Prettier Check (skip in `quick` mode)]

```bash
npm run format:check
```

If files are unformatted, list them. The project uses `.prettierrc` and `.prettierignore` for formatting rules. Suggest running `npm run format` to fix.

[Pre-PR Verification > Pipeline > Step 4: Playwright Tests (only in `pre-pr` mode)]

Find test files changed on this branch vs develop:

```bash
git diff --name-only origin/develop...HEAD -- 'tests/**/*.test.ts'
```

If `--test=<file>` is specified in `$ARGUMENTS`, run that specific test file instead:

```bash
npx playwright test <file>
```

If no test files changed and no `--test` specified, skip this step and note it was skipped.

[Pre-PR Verification > Pipeline > Step 5: Git Status Summary]

```bash
git status
git diff --stat origin/develop...HEAD
```

Show what will be included in the commit/PR — files changed, insertions, deletions.

[Pre-PR Verification > Output]

Produce a concise verification report:

```
VERIFICATION: [PASS/FAIL]

TypeScript:  [OK / X errors]
ESLint:      [OK / X errors, Y warnings]
Prettier:    [OK / X unformatted files]
Tests:       [X passed, Y failed / skipped / not applicable]
Git:         [X files changed, +Y -Z lines]

Ready for PR: [YES/NO]
```

If any step failed, list the issues with fix suggestions below the report.

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework]

This directory contains modular rules for AI assistants working on the Sentinel Playwright automation framework. The rules are organized into focused, maintainable `.mdc` files (Markdown Cursor format) with YAML frontmatter for intelligent rule activation.

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework > 📚 Rules Structure]

The rules are organized in a numbered sequence reflecting their priority and logical flow. Each `.mdc` file contains:

- **YAML frontmatter** with `description`, `globs`, and/or `alwaysApply` settings
- **Markdown content** with the actual rule guidelines

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework > 📚 Rules Structure > Core Foundation (Always Applied)]

- **[00-role-context.mdc](rules/00-role-context.mdc)** - Role definition, project overview, and core principles
- **[01-architecture.mdc](rules/01-architecture.mdc)** - Hybrid POM + Component approach, fixture patterns

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework > 📚 Rules Structure > Coding Standards (Always Applied)]

- **[02-naming-conventions.mdc](rules/02-naming-conventions.mdc)** - File, folder, code, and element naming standards
- **[08-typescript-standards.mdc](rules/08-typescript-standards.mdc)** - TypeScript conventions, type safety, async/await

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework > 📚 Rules Structure > Playwright Best Practices]

- **[03-locators.mdc](rules/03-locators.mdc)** - Locator hierarchy, chart interactions _(applies to `_.page.ts`, `_.component.ts`)_
- **[04-methods-to-avoid.mdc](rules/04-methods-to-avoid.mdc)** - Deprecated/discouraged Playwright methods _(always applied - critical)_
- **[05-assertions.mdc](rules/05-assertions.mdc)** - Auto-retrying vs non-retrying assertions _(applies to `_.test.ts`, `_.page.ts`)_

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework > 📚 Rules Structure > Implementation Patterns]

- **[06-page-objects.mdc](rules/06-page-objects.mdc)** - Page Object structure, component templates _(applies to `_.page.ts`, `_.section.ts`)_
- **[07-test-structure.mdc](rules/07-test-structure.mdc)** - Test organization, reliability principles _(applies to `_.test.ts`)\*

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework > 📚 Rules Structure > Configuration & Workflow]

- **[09-configuration.mdc](rules/09-configuration.mdc)** - Playwright config, environment variables _(applies to `_.config.ts`)\*
- **[10-git-workflow.mdc](rules/10-git-workflow.mdc)** - Git Flow, branching, commit messages _(agent-decided)_

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework > 📚 Rules Structure > Quality & Examples]

- **[11-code-quality.mdc](rules/11-code-quality.mdc)** - Quality checklist, review standards _(agent-decided)_
- **[12-common-patterns.mdc](rules/12-common-patterns.mdc)** - Authentication, modals, navigation patterns _(applies to `_.test.ts`)\*

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework > 🎯 Quick Reference > When to Reference Which Rule]

| Working On...   | Primary Rule(s) | Supporting Rules |
| --------------- | --------------- | ---------------- |
| New page object | 01, 06, 02      | 03, 08           |
| New component   | 01, 06, 02      | 03, 08           |
| Writing tests   | 07, 05, 03      | 04, 12           |
| Test failures   | 07, 03, 05      | 04               |
| Locator issues  | 03              | 04, 05           |
| Code review     | 11              | All              |
| Git/commits     | 10              | -                |

[Cursor Rules - Sentinel Automation Framework > 📖 MDC File Format]

Each rule file uses the Cursor `.mdc` format:

```yaml
---
description: 'Clear description for AI to determine relevance'
globs:
  - '**/*.page.ts'
  - '**/*.component.ts'
alwaysApply: false
---

[Rule content in Markdown...]

```

[Rule content in Markdown... > Activation Types]

| Setting                         | When Applied                    |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
| `alwaysApply: true`             | Every interaction, all files    |
| `globs: [patterns]`             | When editing matching files     |
| `alwaysApply: false` (no globs) | AI decides based on description |

[Rule content in Markdown... > 🚀 Usage for AI Assistants]

When assisting with Sentinel framework development:

1. **Load context from relevant rule files** based on the task
2. **Reference `docs/best-practices/`** for detailed explanations
3. **Apply DAMP principle** - Descriptive And Meaningful Phrases
4. **Prioritize test reliability** over brevity
5. **Follow established patterns** in the codebase

[Rule content in Markdown... > 🛠️ Maintenance > Adding New Rules]

1. Create a new numbered `.mdc` file in `rules/`
2. Add YAML frontmatter with appropriate `description`, `globs`, or `alwaysApply`
3. Update this CURSOR.md index
4. Cross-reference with related rules

[Rule content in Markdown... > 🛠️ Maintenance > Updating Rules]

1. Update the specific rule file
2. Check for cross-references in other rules
3. Update `docs/best-practices/` if the pattern has changed

description: Use this agent when you need to debug and fix failing Playwright tests.
tools: ['edit/createFile', 'edit/createDirectory', 'edit/editFiles', 'search/fileSearch', 'search/textSearch', 'search/listDirectory', 'search/readFile', 'playwright-test/browser_console_messages', 'playwright-test/browser_evaluate', 'playwright-test/browser_generate_locator', 'playwright-test/browser_network_requests', 'playwright-test/browser_snapshot', 'playwright-test/test_debug', 'playwright-test/test_list', 'playwright-test/test_run']
---

You are the Playwright Test Healer, an expert test automation engineer specializing in debugging and
resolving Playwright test failures. Your mission is to systematically identify, diagnose, and fix
broken Playwright tests using a methodical approach.

Your workflow:
1. **Initial Execution**: Run all tests using playwright_test_run_test tool to identify failing tests
2. **Debug failed tests**: For each failing test run playwright_test_debug_test.
3. **Error Investigation**: When the test pauses on errors, use available Playwright MCP tools to:
   - Examine the error details
   - Capture page snapshot to understand the context
   - Analyze selectors, timing issues, or assertion failures
4. **Root Cause Analysis**: Determine the underlying cause of the failure by examining:
   - Element selectors that may have changed
   - Timing and synchronization issues
   - Data dependencies or test environment problems
   - Application changes that broke test assumptions
5. **Code Remediation**: Edit the test code to address identified issues, focusing on:
   - Updating selectors to match current application state
   - Fixing assertions and expected values
   - Improving test reliability and maintainability
   - For inherently dynamic data, utilize regular expressions to produce resilient locators
6. **Verification**: Restart the test after each fix to validate the changes
7. **Iteration**: Repeat the investigation and fixing process until the test passes cleanly

- For inherently dynamic data, utilize regular expressions to produce resilient locators 6. **Verification**: Restart the test after each fix to validate the changes 7. **Iteration**: Repeat the investigation and fixing process until the test passes cleanly

Key principles:
- Be systematic and thorough in your debugging approach
- Document your findings and reasoning for each fix
- Prefer robust, maintainable solutions over quick hacks
- Use Playwright best practices for reliable test automation
- If multiple errors exist, fix them one at a time and retest
- Provide clear explanations of what was broken and how you fixed it
- You will continue this process until the test runs successfully without any failures or errors.
- If the error persists and you have high level of confidence that the test is correct, mark this test as test.fixme()
  so that it is skipped during the execution. Add a comment before the failing step explaining what is happening instead
  of the expected behavior.
- Do not ask user questions, you are not interactive tool, do the most reasonable thing possible to pass the test.
- Never wait for networkidle or use other discouraged or deprecated apis
